what_it_does: Acceptance test for a single shared session:// sub-address grammar. It imports parse_session_address and is_step_address from contracts.address, asserts the two legal session:// forms parse correctly, asserts malformed or unknown sub-addresses raise ValueError, closes the previously divergent edge case session://a/b/step/c, and proves behavior-equivalence to runtime.cc_gate's STEP_ADDR_RE as a pre-swap safety proof.
observations: Self-contained script with a PASS/FAIL accumulator and sys.exit(1) on failure, run directly via Python.
Imports functions that do not yet exist as an intentional falsify-first / RED-before-build step.
Tests both whole-session (session://<sid>) and step (session://<sid>/step/<tuid>) forms.
Explicitly checks that session://a/b/step/c raises, documenting the drift closure between cognition.py's inline split and cc_gate's regex.
Conditional equivalence check against runtime.cc_gate.STEP_ADDR_RE: if the regex is retired, the test passes by construction.
Uses plain assert-style helpers rather than pytest or unittest.
Docstring links the f1ade750 ticket, explains the file naming choice, and declares contracts/address.py as the grammar home.
standouts: The test is designed to fail (RED) before the grammar functions are built, acting as an executable spec.
It encodes the cc_gate regex swap as a pre-swap equivalence proof and a post-swap one-parser-by-construction transition.
It treats the divergent edge case session://a/b/step/c as a must-fail case to close the two-parser drift.
conventions: Uses sys.path insertion to locate the repo root and performs late imports.
Implements a minimal PASS/FAIL harness with check and raises helpers.
Heavy docstring rationale tying the file to a ticket, naming collision avoidance, and future grammar extensions.
Follows the session_address_* naming convention to avoid collision with tests/address_grammar_acceptance.py.
concerns: Direct sys.path manipulation and late imports obscure the real dependency structure.
The equivalence battery is small and hand-picked, not exhaustive over all malformed inputs.
If contracts.address functions are missing, the script fails at import time before any checks run, which is intentional but not graceful.
The check helper prints results but does not capture exception details for failing assertions.
notes: A RED-before-build acceptance harness that pins the session:// address grammar and proves it can safely replace cc_gate's separate regex.
questions: What is the exact implementation of parse_session_address and is_step_address in contracts.address?
What does runtime.cc_gate.STEP_ADDR_RE match and what edge cases did it reject?
How will cognition.py and cc_gate be rewired to use the shared parser?
Are clone://, member://, and mind:// sub-addresses already specified or only reserved as future entries?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to declare and prove one shared session:// grammar; the file does exactly that by testing parsing, failure modes, drift closure, and regex equivalence.
intention_signals: The file is trying to act as an executable contract/spec that forces a unified session:// parser into contracts.address and makes the cc_gate regex swap provably safe before it happens.
novelty: It explicitly encodes the pre-swap equivalence proof and the post-swap 'one parser by construction' transition as a single conditional test.
contribution: A runnable acceptance test that pins the legal session:// forms, mandates loud failure on malformed sub-addresses, documents the f1ade750 drift closure, and provides a swap-safe equivalence check for migrating cc_gate to the shared grammar.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test for contracts.address session:// sub-address grammar covering legal forms, loud failures, drift closure, and cc_gate regex equivalence.
intention_for_embedding: Provide a falsify-first, behavior-preserving acceptance harness for unifying session:// address parsing under contracts.address.

symbols.does: Records a named assertion in PASS or FAIL and prints a PASS/FAIL line.
symbols.description: The check helper takes a test name, a boolean condition, and an optional detail string. It appends the name to the PASS or FAIL list and prints a formatted result line, serving as the minimal assertion harness for this standalone script.

symbols.does: Returns True if calling fn raises the expected exception, optionally containing a substring.
symbols.description: The raises helper executes a zero-argument callable inside a try/except, checking for a specific exception type and optional message substring. It is used throughout the test to assert that malformed or non-session addresses fail loudly.
